qa_pairs.question: What are the names of the application processes and their associated deadlines?
qa_pairs.answer: The application processes are Resources for Research Groups (RRG) and Research Platforms and Portals (RPP). Both applications start on September 23, 2025, and have a deadline of November 4, 2025, at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Standard Time.

qa_pairs.question: Can the application deadline for the Resource Allocation Competition be extended?
qa_pairs.answer: No, an extension of the deadline for the Resource Allocation Competition is not possible.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: The Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) enables faculty members and their research groups to access compute, storage, and cloud resources beyond what can be obtained via the Rapid Access Service (RAS).

qa_pairs.question: Who is responsible for overseeing the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: The Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) is overseen by the Resource Access Program Administrative Committee (RAPAC), which includes representatives from all regional partners and each of the national system host sites.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I plan to request GPU and/or cloud resources?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are planning to request GPU and/or cloud resources, you should attend one of the corresponding information sessions.

qa_pairs.question: What is recommended for first-time applicants to the Resource Allocation Competition?
qa_pairs.answer: First-time applicants are strongly encouraged to schedule a pre-submission consultation before October 31.

qa_pairs.question: What is an important requirement regarding the application template?
qa_pairs.answer: You must use the most up-to-date version of the application template, which is provided in this guide. Failing to do so may result in the rejection of your application.

qa_pairs.question: Where can applicants find information on the evaluation criteria for the competition?
qa_pairs.answer: Applicants can consult the evaluation criteria available in Appendix A and Appendix B of the document.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find additional resources or help with technical terms related to the competition?
qa_pairs.answer: You may consult the RAC Frequently Asked Questions page and the Technical glossary for the resource allocation competitions.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I email if I'm unsure which application process is best for my project?
qa_pairs.answer: If you don't know which application process is best for your project, you should email allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of the Resources for Research Groups (RRG) application process?
qa_pairs.answer: The RRG is a peer-reviewed application process for projects whose primary purpose is to conduct research requiring compute, storage, and cloud resources to meet their goals.

qa_pairs.question: When should projects apply through the RPP application process instead of RRG?
qa_pairs.answer: Projects primarily needing persistent instances in the cloud to provide a service through a platform or a portal should apply through the RPP application process instead of RRG.

qa_pairs.question: What is the RRG Fast Track Application Process?
qa_pairs.answer: The RRG Fast Track Application Process allows users with an existing RRG award who meet the Fast Track eligibility to submit a lightweight progress report to renew their request for computational resources.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible for the RRG Fast Track application?
qa_pairs.answer: Users with an existing RRG award who meet the Fast Track eligibility are allowed to submit a lightweight progress report through the RRG Fast Track application process.

qa_pairs.question: How will Principal Investigators (PIs) be informed about their RRG Fast Track eligibility?
qa_pairs.answer: Principal Investigators (PIs) with an active allocation awarded through the RRG process will receive an email in September indicating whether they are eligible to Fast Track or not.

qa_pairs.question: What are the start and end dates for RRG Fast Track submissions?
qa_pairs.answer: Fast Track submissions start on September 23 and are due November 4, 2025, at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Standard Time.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of the Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) application process?
qa_pairs.answer: The RPP is a peer-reviewed application process for projects whose primary purpose is to provide a service through scientific gateways that improve access to shared datasets, enhance existing online research tools and facilities, or advance national or international research collaborations.

qa_pairs.question: When should projects primarily needing compute resources in a cluster for research apply through the RRG process?
qa_pairs.answer: Projects primarily needing compute resources in a cluster to conduct research should apply through the RRG process instead of RPP.

qa_pairs.question: How is a research platform or portal defined in this document?
qa_pairs.answer: A research platform or portal is defined as a set of community-developed tools, applications, and data that are integrated via a gateway or a suite of applications (usually in a graphical user interface) and customized to meet the needs of a community of users associated with a specific discipline.

qa_pairs.question: What types of resources are typically involved in RPP projects?
qa_pairs.answer: RPP projects typically involve cloud resources, usually through the development of a front-end gateway on persistent virtual machines, with possible backend compute either through cloud compute nodes or job-based submission to the national clusters. Many platforms and portals also include large databases.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two main requirements for projects applying through the RPP process?
qa_pairs.answer: Projects applying through RPP must: 1) provide a service to a larger research community via a set of cloud-based tools, applications, and/or data, thus enabling them to access national computational resources via a common interface; and 2) be able to develop, operate, and manage the proposed portal or platform with minimal support from the Federation.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my multi-year RPP award expires in 2026?
qa_pairs.answer: If your multi-year RPP award expires in 2026, you must submit a new RPP application this fall if your need for resources will continue for the 2026-2027 allocation cycle.

qa_pairs.question: For how many years can RPP allocations be awarded?
qa_pairs.answer: RPP allocations may be awarded over multiple years, with a maximum of three years, subject to an annual review and availability of resources.

qa_pairs.question: Do awarded multi-year RPP projects need to submit a new application every year?
qa_pairs.answer: No, awarded multi-year RPP projects do not need to submit a new application every year, but they are required to complete an annual progress report.

qa_pairs.question: How will a PI be notified about the requirements for the RPP Annual Progress Report?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI will be notified by email with instructions for the information required and the submission process for the RPP Annual Progress Report.

qa_pairs.question: When are RPP Annual Progress Reports due?
qa_pairs.answer: Submissions of the annual RPP Progress Report start September 23 and are due November 4, 2025.
